methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Collaborative Robotics is a Series B-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Brad Porter, currently valued at $500M+ post-money on $150M+ raised. The valuation assessment draws from a peer universe of nine comparable companies—one public traded entity and eight private firms at equivalent or adjacent stages, including 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Fairmat, and GrayMatter Robotics.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applied a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $500M last post-money valuation, yielding $950M. Peer-median benchmarking across five same-sector, same-stage comparables produced a median valuation of $1.6B, with a wide dispersion ranging from $300M (GrayMatter Robotics, Fairmat) to $10.0B (1X Technologies). These two methods generated an implied range from $808M to $1.9B, with a median of $1.3B.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $800M to $1.9B, centering near $1.3B. Key uncertainties include the substantial scatter in peer valuations—a 33x spread from floor to ceiling—which reflects divergent risk profiles, technology maturity, and market positioning within robotics. The current $500M+ valuation sits below both the stage-progression and peer-median indications, suggesting either relative undervaluation or heightened execution risk relative to the peer cohort.
